rationale: The page provides a comprehensive overview of Geospatial Technology, including its definition, core components (GIS, GPS, Remote Sensing, and Cartography), and detailed information on the National Geospatial Policy 2022 and Operation Dronagiri. This factual and structured content is highly suitable for generating multiple-choice or descriptive questions.
cleanedText: 8. MISCELLANEOUS. 8.1. GEOSPATIAL TECHNOLOGY. Concept Corner: Geospatial Technology. Geospatial technology refers to a set of tools and techniques used to collect, analyze, and visualize spatial and geographic data. Components of Geospatial Technology: 1. Geographic Information System (GIS): Captures, stores, and analyzes spatial data. Helps in mapping and visualization of geographic features. Used in urban planning, agriculture, and disaster response. 2. Global Positioning System (GPS): Uses satellite signals to determine precise locations. Essential for navigation, transportation, and military operations. 3. Remote Sensing (RS): Collects data from satellites and aerial platforms. Monitors environmental changes, land use, and natural disasters. E.g. LiDAR. 4. Cartography and Spatial Analysis: Represents geographic data in maps. Aids in policy-making and resource management. 8.1.1. OPERATION DRONAGIRI. Why in the News? Haryana has constituted state and district-level committees for the implementation of Operation Dronagiri. About Operation Dronagiri: A pilot project under National Geospatial Policy 2022, and will be implemented by Geospatial Innovation Cell, Department of Science and Technology. Purpose: To demonstrate the potential applications of geospatial technologies and innovations in improving the quality of life of citizens and ease of doing business. First Phase Implementation: In the states of UP, Haryana, Assam, Andhra Pradesh & Maharashtra. Focus is on Agriculture, Livelihoods, Logistics and Transport, Urban Planning and Infrastructure. Development of an Integrated Geospatial Data Sharing Interface (GDI). National Geospatial Policy 2022: Vision: Position India as global leader in geospatial sector, develop national framework, and enable availability of geospatial data. Institutional Framework: Policy provided for establishment of Geospatial Data Promotion and Development Committee as apex body for formulating guidelines and programs for promotion of Geospatial sector. Nodal Agency: Survey of India (SoI).
